warnings: Accepted atomic coupled package with 3 edits; gate preserved package coherence.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Increasing the terminal reward alone could lead to excessively high total reward and instability; decreasing shaping alone could make learning too sparse. The adjustments form an interdependent rebalancing package that must be applied as one unit.
editor_constraints: Ensure the stable_landing event reward, when multiplied by the maximum possible occurrences (1), significantly exceeds the cumulative shaping reward a bouncing agent would collect during a typical episode.
Maintain the structure of shaping components (proximity, stability, landing_quality) but reduce their per‑step multiplicative weights so that they guide learning without dominating the end goal.
Do not remove any component entirely – at least one proximity/guidance component must remain active.
If the stable_landing condition (duration_steps=5) is relaxed, keep it >= 3 steps to avoid rewarding momentary contacts.
r_energy penalty should stay small and not suppress necessary control actions.
must_preserve: r_approach_region (or an equivalent proximity shaping signal)
One‑time triggers for safe_contact and stable_landing to prevent repeated exploitation
must_avoid: Adding per‑contact or toggle‑based rewards that could incentivise bouncing
Purely sparse reward (removal of all shaping)
Increasing r_energy so much that it prevents the agent from taking required control actions
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: Shift reward dominance from approach_region to the terminal stable landing event; reduce repeated bouncing/loitering; increase the rate of stable landings and lower episode reward variance.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: Over‑weighting the terminal event could cause the agent to crash while trying to land quickly; over‑suppressing shaping could stall early learning. The rebalancing must be monitored for crash rate and learning curve. A temporary increase in episode reward variance is possible during adaptation.
